rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Peek at the next module before adding a second exercise
Between-module reading appears in two places
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No M4-bound between-module readings exist for the M3→M4 gap. M3's optional pre-reads (Willison lethal-trifecta, OWASP) sit in the M2→M3 gap and appear in M3 Prework only (M2 is out of this audit scope). M4's only Prework item is the scoped task (an artefact, not a reading). M4's `## Pre-reads before Module 5` are M5-bound. Rule does not fire on this pair.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts must be explicit at both ends
Session boundaries and working directories are designed, not implicit
rules_evaluated.evidence: JUDGE-OVER-FIRE corrected 2026-05-15: The first M4 exercise `walk-and-send-off.md` literally opens with `**Session** *(new, "Module 4 - Run the first experiment")*` followed by `Start a new Claude Code session at your repo root.` — both session-shape AND working-directory are explicit at the first-exercise layer (the canonical session-open syntax for AE101). The module file's `## Start here` framing-and-question is the trainer-cohort cue layer; the session widget at exercise-top is the cwd/session layer. Rule 4 fully satisfied; judge missed the link-walked exercise content.

maintainer_acceptance.reason: Cwd is literal at walk-and-send-off.md exercise-top: 'Start a new Claude Code session at your repo root.' Adding the same line to the module file is belt-and-suspenders for an ambiguity that doesn't exist. Antti 2026-05-15.

rules_evaluated.fix_hint: M3 does not end with an autonomous send-off / unattended run that M4 load-bears on. M3 closes with student-authored artefacts (skill, ADR, CLAUDE.local.md) all produced interactively. M4's load-bearing inputs are the scoped task (student picks at home) plus the M3 artefacts — none of which can plausibly be thin/missing in the way an autonomous run can. Rule's trigger condition (prior module's send-off / autonomous run that can plausibly be thin) does not apply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit-discipline — walk M(N-1) close before flagging M(N)
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Meta-rule for the auditor, not a per-file finding. No architecture-line spec-drift claim under evaluation in this pair.
